response:
If your submissions are not working, or if challenges do not load or show an infinite processing message, please try the following steps:
1. Close the challenge window, then reopen it using the same challenge link (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/practice-coding-challenges/1273631973-coding-challenges-faqs.md).
2. Open the challenge in another supported browser, such as the latest versions of Chrome, Firefox, or Edge, for full compatibility (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/practice-coding-challenges/1273631973-coding-challenges-faqs.md).
3. Ensure your internet connection is stable (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/practice-coding-challenges/1273631973-coding-challenges-faqs.md).

If you do not see your challenge results after submission, please:
1. Wait for the challenge results to process. They usually appear shortly after submission (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/practice-coding-challenges/1273631973-coding-challenges-faqs.md).
2. Check the leaderboard, as it may take time to refresh (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/practice-coding-challenges/1273631973-coding-challenges-faqs.md).
3. Confirm you are logged into the same HackerRank Community account you used to participate in the challenge, as results do not appear across different accounts (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/practice-coding-challenges/1273631973-coding-challenges-faqs.md).

The provided documentation does not contain information regarding an 'apply tab'. For further assistance, please contact help@hackerrank.com or support@hackerrank.com.
justification: The response provides troubleshooting steps for submission issues and challenges not loading, referencing the Coding Challenges FAQs. It also notes that information about an 'apply tab' is not available in the provided documentation.

response:
If you are experiencing issues with submissions not working or challenge results not appearing, please try the following troubleshooting steps:

1.  Close the challenge window, then reopen it using the same challenge link (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/practice-coding-challenges/1273631973-coding-challenges-faqs.md).
2.  Open the challenge in another supported browser, such as the latest versions of Chrome, Firefox, or Edge, for full compatibility (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/practice-coding-challenges/1273631973-coding-challenges-faqs.md).
3.  Ensure your internet connection is stable (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/practice-coding-challenges/1273631973-coding-challenges-faqs.md).
4.  Wait for the challenge results to process. Challenge results usually appear shortly after you submit a solution. If your result does not appear immediately, it may still be processing (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/practice-coding-challenges/1273631973-coding-challenges-faqs.md).
5.  Confirm you are using the correct account. Log in to the same HackerRank Community account you used to participate in the challenge, as results do not appear across different accounts (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/practice-coding-challenges/1273631973-coding-challenges-faqs.md).

Please note that if you take part in a company-sponsored challenge, HackerRank does not display the results; the company that hosts the challenge contacts you directly with the results or next steps (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/practice-coding-challenges/1273631973-coding-challenges-faqs.md).

For further assistance, please contact help@hackerrank.com or support@hackerrank.com.
justification: The user is reporting an issue with submissions not working and results not appearing. The provided documentation offers specific troubleshooting steps for these issues, including checking browser, internet connection, account, and waiting for processing. It also clarifies the policy for company-sponsored challenges. The response compiles these steps and advises contacting support if the issue persists.

response:
You can pause your HackerRank subscription if you are an individual self-serve plan subscriber with an active monthly subscription (Individual Monthly, Basic, or Interview Monthly) that started at least 30 days ago. To do so, follow these steps:
1. Click on your profile icon in the top-right corner and select **Settings**.
2. Navigate to the **Billing** section under **Subscription**.
3. Click the **Cancel Plan** button. A prompt about the **Pause Subscription** feature will appear.
4. Select your desired pause duration (minimum 1 month, maximum 12 months).
5. Click **Confirm Pause**.

This information is based on the "Pause Subscription" documentation (hackerrank/settings/user-account-settings-and-preferences/5157311476-pause-subscription.md). The documentation specifies this feature for individual self-serve plans. If your subscription is not an individual self-serve plan, these steps may not apply. For further assistance, please contact help@hackerrank.com or support@hackerrank.com.
justification: The user requested to pause their subscription, and the documentation provides clear steps and prerequisites for pausing an individual self-serve HackerRank subscription. The response outlines these steps and notes the scope limitation of the documentation.

response:
If Claude has stopped working completely and all requests are failing, there are a few potential causes based on the documentation:

1.  **Usage Limits:** If your account is configured for extra usage and you have exceeded your set spend limit, you will be unable to use Claude, Cowork, or Claude Code until the next billing period or until your limits are adjusted (claude/team-and-enterprise-plans/admin-management/12005970-manage-extra-usage-for-team-and-seat-based-enterprise-plans.md).
2.  **Desktop App Session Persistence:** If you are using the Claude Desktop app, especially for Cowork tasks, ensure the app remains open and your computer is awake. If the app is closed or your computer goes to sleep, active tasks will stop (claude/claude/features-and-capabilities/13345190-get-started-with-claude-cowork.md).
3.  **VM Service Not Running (for Cowork on Windows):** If you are seeing a "VM service not running" message when trying to start Cowork on Windows, it indicates the Claude VM Service (CoworkVMService) is unavailable. This can be resolved by reinstalling from the download page or starting "Claude VM Service" in services.msc (claude/claude/features-and-capabilities/13345190-get-started-with-claude-cowork.md).

For further assistance, please contact support@anthropic.com or visit support.claude.com.
justification: The response addresses the user's issue of Claude not responding and all requests failing by providing troubleshooting steps related to usage limits and desktop app session persistence, directly from the provided documentation.

Yes, you can update the name on your certificate, but only once per account. This change will apply to all your certificates. To update your name, please follow these steps:

4. In the confirmation dialog, click Update Name.

response:
Thank you for reporting a security vulnerability. Anthropic prioritizes the security of our systems and user data. To report a security vulnerability, please review our Responsible Disclosure Policy, which includes a section on how to submit a report (claude/safeguards/11427875-public-vulnerability-reporting.md).

If the vulnerability you found is a universal jailbreak, which is a generalized technique that reliably elicits policy-violating responses from a language model, you may be interested in our Model Safety Bug Bounty Program. This program is run through HackerOne and focuses on identifying universal jailbreaks in our deployed systems (claude/safeguards/12119250-model-safety-bug-bounty-program.md).
justification: Provided instructions for reporting security vulnerabilities and information about the bug bounty program, directly addressing the user's request for next steps.

response:
To stop Claude from crawling your website, you need to modify the `robots.txt` file in your top-level directory. Add the following lines to block the ClaudeBot from your entire website:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

YouPlease do this for every subdomain you wish to opt out from. Anthropic's Bots respect "do not crawl" signals by honoring industry standard directives in `robots.txt` (claude/privacy-and-legal/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler.md).

If you have further questions or believe our Bots may be malfunctioning, you can reach out to claudebot@anthropic.com. Please ensure your email includes the domain you are contacting us about for verification purposes (claude/privacy-and-legal/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler.md).

For further assistance, please contact support@anthropic.com or visit support.claude.com.
justification: The user's request to stop website crawling is directly addressed by the provided documentation on modifying robots.txt and contacting the specific email for bot-related issues.

For customer support inquiries related to using Claude in Amazon Bedrock, you should contact AWS Support or reach out to your AWS account manager (claude/amazon-bedrock/7996921-i-use-claude-in-amazon-bedrock-who-do-i-contact-for-customer-support-inquiries.md). response:
To set up the Claude LTI key for your students in Canvas, you will need to follow these steps, which are intended for Claude for Education administrators and Learning Management Systems (LMS) administrators (claude/claude-for-education/11725453-set-up-the-claude-lti-in-canvas-by-instructure.md):

1.  **Creating Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas:**
    *   Sign in to Canvas as an administrator and navigate to **Admin -> Developer Keys**.
    *   Click "+ Developer Key" then "+ LTI Key."
    *   Enter the required information: Key Name (Claude LTI), Description, Redirect URIs (https://claude.ai/lti/launch), Title (Claude LTI), Target Link URI (https://claude.ai/lti/launch), OpenID Connect Initiation Url (https://claude.ai/api/lti/login), and JWK method (https://claude.ai/api/lti/keys).
    *   Under **Additional Settings**, toggle Privacy Level to **Public**.
    *   Under **Placements**, add "Course Navigation" and "Assignment Edit" as options.
    *   Click "Save" and toggle the state to **On**.

2.  **Installing Claude LTI as an App:**
    *   In Canvas, go to **Admin -> Settings -> Apps**.
    *   Click "View App Configurations" then select "+ App."
    *   Select **Configuration Type** “By Client ID.”
    *   Input the Client ID generated for your developer key (from Step 6 under Creating Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas).
    *   Click "Install" and refresh the course page.

3.  **Turn on the Claude LTI Integration in Claude for Education organization settings:**
    *   In Claude for Education, sign in as an administrator.
    *   Navigate to **Organization settings > Connectors** (https://claude.ai/admin-settings/connectors).
    *   Find **Canvas** and click "Enable."
    *   In the settings modal, input the required information: **Canvas Domain**, **Client ID** (found in Canvas Admin -> Developer Keys), and **Deployment ID** (found in Canvas Admin -> Settings -> Apps -> View App Configurations -> Claude LTI Settings Button -> Deployment ID).
    *   Click "Save Changes." The integration should now show as enabled.

If you have any questions about your Claude for Education plan account or the Claude LTI, we encourage you to contact your university’s administrator(s) (claude/claude-for-education/11725453-set-up-the-claude-lti-in-canvas-by-instructure.md). For further assistance, please contact support@anthropic.com or visit support.claude.com.
justification: The user is asking for instructions on setting up Claude LTI for students. The provided documentation 'claude/claude-for-education/11725453-set-up-the-claude-lti-in-canvas-by-instructure.md' directly addresses this request with detailed steps for Canvas LMS administrators.
